rationale: Ground truth's substantive concern (dsocek's suggestion to derive component/name mapping from `_packed_modules_mapping`/`stacked_params_mapping` rather than hardcoded lists) isn't hit precisely by either candidate, but X's comment #1 about consolidating hardcoded component lists into a single source of truth (`_dit_modules`) is conceptually adjacent and shows real investigative grounding with specific file/line citations; X also flags a concrete, plausible follow-up gap (text-encoder LoRA silent no-op). Y merely re-validates that the diff is correct and recommends APPROVE with zero critical or forward-looking findings, missing all reviewer concerns and offering nothing actionable. X's comments are concrete and pinpointed while Y's are purely confirmatory.
